notes: Source: docs/ghl-reference/acquisitions/acquisitions-tags.csv. Lowercase exactly. opt-in, lead-new, uw-requested, pkg-requested are critical workflow triggers — must match seller-side function expectations.

tags.name: seller
tags.purpose: Marks contact as seller (analog of TFS 'tfs buyer'). Set on every seller-side intake.

tags.name: agent
tags.purpose: Submitter checked is_licensed_agent. Licensed agent submitter.

tags.name: opt in
tags.purpose: Required gate before any marketing SMS/email (mirrors TFS opt-in rule).

tags.name: acquisition-source-website
tags.purpose: Came in via termsforsale.com seller form.

tags.name: lead-stale
tags.purpose: No response in 7 days. Trigger for follow-up-nudge.

tags.name: lead-dead
tags.purpose: Manual disposition or 30+ days no response.

tags.name: call-prepped
tags.purpose: Brief is ready for Eddie's outbound call.

tags.name: call-completed
tags.purpose: Eddie marked call done in GHL.

tags.name: birddog-approved
tags.purpose: Promoted to lead-new for full intake.

tags.name: migrated:acq
tags.purpose: One-shot tag during sub-account split. Identifies contacts moved from TFS to ACQ (rollback safety net).

rules: lead-new is the canonical entry point — every seller-side intake function applies it.
Tier tags (lead-warm / lead-hot) are mutually exclusive — swapTags should remove the other when changing tiers.
opt in is REQUIRED on a contact before any campaign SMS/email per CLAUDE.md global rule.
uw-requested → uw-complete → call-prepped is the seller pipeline core; never skip stages.
